heroDesc: For parts requiring identification, traceability, assembly direction or branding.
bodyDesc: Permanent serial numbers, QR codes, logos and traceability marks for prototype and batch parts. Actual results depend on material grade, pre-treatment, geometry, thickness, masking and acceptance criteria, so these details should be confirmed during quoting.

panels.title: Drawing input
panels.subtitle: Material + finish notes
panels.body: State color, thickness, masked faces, cosmetic level and inspection standard.

panels.title: Quality check
panels.subtitle: Appearance / size / adhesion
panels.body: Appearance, thickness, hardness or corrosion test can be confirmed by process type.

panels.title: Risk control
panels.subtitle: Review boundaries first
panels.body: Deep holes, sharp edges, thin walls, threads and precision fits need early review.

tableRows: QR code, serial number, logo and direction mark
Vector or clear artwork files are recommended.
Avoid fit faces and high-wear surfaces
Durability depends on material and mark depth.

asideItems.label: Main goal
asideItems.value: Serial numbers, QR codes and traceability marks.

asideCta.title: Need finishing advice?
asideCta.body: Share material, appearance, function and masking requirements so engineering can match a suitable process.

related.title: Related Finishes
related.desc: Finishes differ in appearance, thickness, corrosion behavior, wear behavior and cost, so they should be compared against part use.

items.title: Anodizing
items.body: A common aluminum finish that improves corrosion resistance, wear behavior and appearance options such as clear or black.

items.title: Electroplating
items.body: Zinc, nickel, chrome, tin and other plating routes improve corrosion resistance, conductivity, wear or appearance.

items.title: Painting
items.body: Supports color, gloss, insulating protection and masked areas on metal and selected plastic parts.

faq.title: Laser Marking FAQ
faq.desc: These answers help clarify fit, input files and quotation review before submitting a manufacturing request.

items.q: What projects is Laser Marking suitable for?
items.a: Laser Marking is suitable for projects that need manufacturing review based on drawings, material, quantity, tolerances and delivery goals, especially prototypes, low-volume parts and functional components.

items.q: What information is needed for a Laser Marking quote?
items.a: Prepare 3D files, 2D drawings when tolerances matter, material grade, quantity, finish requirements, inspection needs and target delivery timing so engineers can evaluate the route accurately.

items.q: How are lead time and cost confirmed for Laser Marking?
items.a: Lead time and cost are confirmed after reviewing geometry, material, process route, finishing, inspection scope and order quantity. Complex tolerances or special finishes may require engineering confirmation.

ctaDesc: Share drawings and requirements — our engineers will recommend a stable process route.
